Article Title: Sophie Cunningham shares eye-catching beach moments with 6-foot-2 Fever teammate
Article Text:
Sophie Cunningham and Lexie Hull. Image via: Kate Frese/ NBAE via Getty Images

Sophie Cunningham turns heads in bikini beach photos with Fever teammate Lexie Hull

Fans react to Sophie Cunningham and Lexie Hull's beach moment

Sophie Cunningham's Fever status

Sophie Cunningham. Image via: Erica Denhoff/ Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

Sophie Cunningham and Lexie Hull enjoyed a relaxing day at Manhattan Beach during the Indiana Fever's West Coast road trip, offering fans a glimpse into their close friendship ahead of Wednesday's WNBA game against the Los Angeles Sparks. The beach outing came after Indiana's convincing 84-68 victory over the Las Vegas Aces, giving the team a brief opportunity to recharge before returning to action.The social media post quickly gained attention among WNBA fans, not only because of the pair's beach photos but also because Cunningham and Hull have become two of the Fever's most recognizable personalities off the court.Hull shared a selfie with Cunningham on her Instagram Story from Manhattan Beach, California, where both guards spent part of their day off soaking up the sun before Indiana resumed its demanding travel schedule. Cunningham later reshared the image, adding to the buzz surrounding the post.Hull wore a pastel-striped triangle bikini with bright pink trim, while Cunningham opted for a geometric-patterned triangle bikini. The two lay side by side on the sand, enjoying what Hull described in her caption as "much needed r&r."The outing followed another productive performance from both players during Sunday's win over Las Vegas.Cunningham scored nine points off the bench, knocking down three shots from beyond the arc, while Hull continued her strong run as a starter with 10 points, four rebounds and two steals in 22 minutes.The Fever improved to 12-8 with that victory, marking their second straight win after defeating the Los Angeles Sparks 111-87 on June 27. Indiana now sits firmly in the playoff picture while navigating one of the busiest stretches of its schedule.Hull previously appeared on Cunningham's offseason podcast, and Cunningham has often praised her teammate publicly. Together, they have become fan favorites, with many supporters affectionately referring to them as the Fever's "mermaids."The beach selfie quickly circulated across social media after being reposted by fan accounts, prompting hundreds of reactions from supporters.Some fans joked about the security surrounding the players during the outing."I see security is staying out of sight. Lol!" one fan wrote.Another added, "I bet Lex fiancé is there for security."Others focused on the ongoing online conversation surrounding the Fever."lol so now you like these two because you think they aren’t hanging out with Caitlin Clark in that moment? But as soon as they’re back on court backing up their teammate, the racial hate and insults start up again," another user posted.One response defended the lighthearted nature of the interactions between Cunningham and Clark."You don’t seem to grasp the irony or humor. Sophie calls CC pale as a joke. You sound humorless and missing the point."Another fan added a humorous observation."Feel bad for the body guards standing on the beach in suits protecting these two."Cunningham's fanbase is always ready to create a buzz.While Cunningham's social media presence often grabs headlines, her production has been equally valuable for Indiana this season.The veteran guard has quietly delivered one of the best campaigns of her career after re-signing with the Fever during the offseason. Through 19 games, Cunningham is averaging 9.5 points, 2.5 rebounds and 1.4 assists while shooting 49.1 percent from the field and an impressive 42.9 percent from three-point range.Despite those numbers, Cunningham was left off the WNBA All-Star reserve list when the league announced its 12 selections on Tuesday. The omission means the 29-year-old is still searching for her first All-Star appearance after eight seasons.Cunningham has reached double figures nine times this season, including a season-high 24-point performance against the Toronto Tempo on June 16 and a 17-point outing against the Seattle Storm earlier in the year.
Article Summary: Sophie Cunningham and Lexie Hull enjoyed a relaxing day at Manhattan Beach, California. The pair shared a selfie on Instagram, which quickly went viral. The beach outing came after Indiana's 84-68 victory over the Las Vegas Aces. Cunningham and Hull have become two of the Fever's most recognizable personalities.

Article Title: Stephen A. Smith and Draymond Green's feud explained: What sparked the latest beef?
Article Text:
Stephen A. Smith and Draymond Green. Image via: Arturo Holmes/ Getty Images | Justin Ford/Getty Images

How did Stephen A. Smith and Draymond Green's latest feud start?



Stephen A. Smith and Draymond Green have a long history



What happens next between Stephen A. Smith and Draymond Green?



Draymond Green has reignited his long-running friction with Stephen A. Smith, this time questioning whether the ESPN personality deserves to call himself a journalist at all. Green made the comments while appearing on Jemele Hill's podcast this week, tying his criticism directly to how Smith has covered Boston Celtics star Jaylen Brown during trade rumors and reporting built on anonymous sources.The comments arrive during an already chaotic stretch for Brown, who was traded to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and multiple draft picks roughly a week ago. That trade set off a chain reaction across NBA media, and Green's remarks are the latest flashpoint in it.The dispute traces back to how Smith and other media figures characterized Brown around the trade. Reports citing unnamed sources questioned Brown's demeanor and value, prompting pushback from players including LeBron James and Austin Rivers. Brown has separately accused Smith of leaning on "clickbait" tactics after Smith aired an anonymous message criticizing his personality.Green picked up that thread with Hill, arguing Smith invokes the "journalist" label without doing the legwork that comes with it. "He throws out the journalist tag all the time," Green said, adding that real accountability means showing up in person the way a beat writer would. He argued that pattern is part of why athletes have grown skeptical of media credibility across the league.Separately, Green also defended Brown's intelligence after Colin Cowherd suggested on his show that Brown's self-regard bordered on a "disease."Green called that framing absurd, saying Jaylen Brown is "brilliant" and pushing back on the idea that confidence should be treated as a flaw.This isn't the first time the two have clashed. In 2024, Green criticized Smith on Shaquille O'Neal's podcast for how Smith discussed his indefinite suspension after striking Phoenix center Jusuf Nurkic, saying Smith failed to separate the person from the player. Smith fired back on First Take, listing years of on-air support for Green and saying he wished Green had called him privately instead of going public.In 2025, Green weighed in again after LeBron James confronted Smith at a Lakers game over comments about James' son, Bronny. Green called Smith's remarks about fatherhood out of bounds, saying criticizing James "as a father" crossed a line Smith wouldn't cross face to face.Smith has not yet addressed Green's latest comments directly, but the pattern between the two suggests a response is likely given how consistently they've traded public jabs over the past two years. Brown, meanwhile, remains at the center of the broader anonymous-sourcing debate as he begins his new chapter in Philadelphia, and how Smith frames that coverage going forward may determine whether this specific dispute with Green cools down or escalates again.
Article Summary: Draymond Green questioned whether Stephen A. Smith deserves to call himself a journalist. Green said Smith invokes the "journalist" label without doing the legwork that comes with it. Smith has not yet addressed Green's latest comments directly.

Article Title: How to craft and use Straw Bed in Minecraft
Article Text:
Image via Mojang

How to craft a Straw Bed in Minecraft



3 Hay Bales placed in a horizontal row across the crafting grid

How to use the Straw Bed in Minecraft



When should you use a Straw Bed?



Mojang is bringing several new things as part of Minecraft 26.3, with one of the introductions being the Straw Bed. It emerges as one of the most practically useful items for adventurers. Unlike a regular bed, the Straw Bed allows you to sleep through the night without being tied to a new spawn point.This makes the item perfect for long adventures, and you can take it for the purpose of things like cave expeditions and diving. In the section below, we take a look at how you can end up crafting the Straw Bed and using it in Minecraft.The Straw Bed is a new single-use sleeping item that Mojang has ended up introducing as part of Minecraft 26.3 Snapshot 3. It is essentially among the key features of the upcoming Wilderness Drop.The core function of the Straw Bed is straightforward, and it basically allows you to sleep through the night or during a thunderstorm without setting or changing your spawn point.When it comes to crafting a Straw Bed, the ingredients are easy to obtain even in early survival. Here is the recipe:One crafting operation using 3 Hay Bales produces 4 Straw Beds, making it one of the more generous crafting ratios in the game.Hay Bales themselves are crafted from 9 Wheat each, or can be found naturally in villages. Given how accessible Wheat is even in the early game, stocking up on Straw Beds for a big expedition is something any player can do before setting out.Once used, the Straw Bed is destroyed, so unlike a regular bed, you cannot set up a permanent respawn location with it. This makes it fundamentally different from every other bed in the game.Standard beds always set your spawn when you use them. The Straw Bed specifically does not. You are getting the skip-the-night functionality without any of the spawn-point commitment that comes with it.Using a Straw Bed works identically to a regular bed. Place it down on the ground, right-click or interact with it, and if it is night-time or during a thunderstorm, you will sleep through until morning.When you wake up, the Straw Bed is gone, and your spawn point remains completely unchanged.One important limitation to note: Straw Beds cannot be used in the Nether or The End. If you attempt to place and use one in either dimension, it will be destroyed without letting you sleep.The Straw Bed's ideal use cases are clear. If you are on a long exploration run far from your base and you have not built a shelter, a Straw Bed lets you survive the night without permanently relocating your spawn to the middle of nowhere. It is similarly valuable during extended cave runs, where placing a regular bed would shift the respawn deep underground.Carry a stack of Straw Beds on every major adventure, and you will never have to waste time building a temporary shelter or losing sleep over your spawn point again.This was all you needed to know about the Straw Bed in Minecraft.
Article Summary: The Straw Bed is a new single-use sleeping item that Mojang has introduced as part of Minecraft 26.3 Snapshot 3. Unlike a regular bed, the Straw Bed allows you to sleep through the night without being tied to a new spawn point. This makes the item perfect for long adventures, and you can take it for things like cave expeditions and diving.

Article Title: Why did Ed Sheeran secretly visit England's World Cup camp in Kansas? Real reason explained
Article Text:
Ed Sheeran with England team. Image via: England/ YouTube

England receives surprise boost as Ed Sheeran makes secret World Cup camp visit



How the Mexico win set up a Norway showdown

What comes next for England



Stage



Date



Match



Venue



Kick-off



Quarterfinal



Saturday, July 11



England vs. Norway



Miami



10:00 p.m.



Semifinal*



Wednesday, July 15



England vs. Argentina or Switzerland



Atlanta



8:00 p.m.



Final*



Sunday, July 19



England vs. France, Spain, Morocco or Belgium



New Jersey



8:00 p.m.





Ed Sheeran made a secret visit to England's World Cup base in Kansas City last week, performing an acoustic set for Thomas Tuchel's squad days before their 3-2 Round of 16 win over Mexico. The England team released footage of the gig on their app Tuesday, showing the 35-year-old singer playing "Castle on the Hill" for players and staff during downtime.The visit is the latest chapter in a friendship between Sheeran and the squad that goes back five years, and it lands at a pivotal moment as England prepare for a quarterfinal against Norway.Sheeran's appearance was not a one-off. He first joined the squad during the delayed Euro 2020 tournament in 2021, an arrangement captain Harry Kane set up personally. He has returned for major tournaments since, including a visit ahead of England's 2024 European Championship quarterfinal against Switzerland. Sheeran, a lifelong Ipswich Town fan who owns a minority stake in the club, described the relationship as an easy fit with the squad, saying the atmosphere among the players has never felt like "a tough crowd" to perform for.England's path to the quarterfinals has not been simple. They beat DR Congo 2-1 in the Round of 32 despite conceding an early goal, then delivered a statement result against Mexico at Estadio Azteca, becoming the first team ever to beat Mexico in Mexico City at a World Cup.That win booked a quarterfinal date with Norway in Miami on July 12, a matchup that pits Kane, who has scored six goals this tournament, against Norway's Erling Haaland, who has scored seven. Jude Bellingham has added four goals of his own as England chase a first World Cup title since 1966.England flew back to Kansas City on Monday and used the day for recovery before shifting focus to Norway. Sheeran, for his part, is already looking further ahead. Asked to predict the final, he said, "I think it'll be an England-France final." Whether that prediction holds depends on what happens against Haaland and Norway first, a test that will say far more about England's title chances than any pregame singalong.England will face Norway on Saturday night's World Cup quarterfinal, with a potential semifinal showdown against Argentina on the horizon after the defending champions staged a dramatic comeback from 2-0 down to beat Egypt and book their place in the last eight.Check out England's World Cup 2026 fixtures, schedule and potential path to the finals–* If England advances.
Article Summary: England will face Norway in World Cup quarterfinal on Saturday night. Ed Sheeran made a secret visit to England's World Cup base in Kansas City last week. The 35-year-old singer played "Castle on the Hill" for players and staff. England beat Mexico 3-2 in Round of 16 to book quarterfinal date with Norway.

Article Title: Nolan Wells update: What happened to Mississippi teen after Horn Island trip? Family takes new step for investigation
Article Text:
Nolan Wells update: What happened to Mississippi teen after Horn Island trip? Family takes new step for investigation (Image via Getty)

Who was Nolan Xavier Wells and what happened during the Horn Island trip ?

Why has Nolan Xavier Wells’ family hired attorney Ben Crump?

Eighteen-year-old Nolan Xavier Wells, a student-athlete from Ocean Springs, Mississippi, was found dead after going missing during a Fourth of July trip to Horn Island. His family is now seeking answers and has hired civil rights attorney Ben Crump as investigators continue to look into what happened. Authorities have not yet determined a cause of death, and an autopsy was scheduled for July 7. The case has drawn attention across Mississippi as family members, friends, and community members wait for more information about the final hours of the young football player’s life.According to the Jackson County Sheriff’s Department, Wells traveled by boat to Horn Island with a group of friends on July 4 to celebrate Independence Day. Officials said he was last seen on the island at around 3 p.m.When the group returned, Wells was not with them. His mother reported him missing later that night, triggering a large search operation involving local, state, and federal agencies. The search included the U.S. Coast Guard, the Mississippi Department of Marine Resources, and the National Park Service.On the morning of July 6, a U.S. park ranger found a body near the northwestern tip of Horn Island.Wells’ family later confirmed that it was Nolan.Jackson County Sheriff John Ledbetter told The Associated Press that investigators had spoken with people who were with Wells on the island and that they were cooperating with the investigation.“From the people we’ve talked to, it sounds like he chose to stay on the island with the assumption that he was going to ride back to the mainland with someone else,” Ledbetter told The Associated Press.Officials have stressed that the investigation remains active and that no official cause of death has been announced.On July 7, Ben Crump’s law office announced that Wells’ family had retained the nationally known attorney to help them seek answers about the teenager’s death.In a statement released by the legal team, Crump said the family deserves a full and transparent investigation into the circumstances surrounding Wells’ death.“Nolan Wells was a beloved son, teammate and friend who went out to celebrate the Fourth of July and never came home. His family deserves answers. They deserve the truth. We will not rest until every fact about what happened to Nolan on Horn Island is brought into the light, and we call on investigators to pursue this case with the urgency and transparency this family deserves.”The legal team said it plans to conduct an independent review while also pushing for the release of records, witness statements, and autopsy findings.Wells was a football player at Ocean Springs High School and later became a wide receiver at Southwest Mississippi Community College. He was preparing to return for the upcoming season and would have turned 19 next month.His mother, Christine Wonsley, also appealed for information through social media, asking anyone who may have videos or other material from Horn Island to come forward.For now, many questions remain unanswered. Investigators are continuing their work, while Nolan Wells’ family says it is focused on learning exactly what happened during the holiday trip that ended in tragedy.
Article Summary: Nolan Xavier Wells, 18, was found dead on July 6 after going missing during a Fourth of July trip to Horn Island. Authorities have not yet determined a cause of death, and an autopsy was scheduled for July 7. Wells was a football player at Ocean Springs High School and later became a wide receiver at Southwest Mississippi Community College.

Article Title: "Seven absolutely incredible artists": Global Citizen CEO showers praise on BTS and ARMY ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026 halftime show
Article Text:
Global Citizen CEO Hugh Evans thanked BTS and ARMY ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026 final halftime show, revealing fans have already raised over $40,000 for the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund. He praised BTS for using their platform to support children's education worldwide. The historic halftime show on July 19 will feature BTS, Madonna and Shakira, with proceeds helping the campaign's $100 million fundraising goal.

BTS FIFA World Cup 2026 halftime show inspires ARMY to support children's education

BTS joins Shakira and Madonna for historic FIFA World Cup 2026 halftime show

One of the biggest talking-points ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026 final comes from a simple social media post. As excitement builds for BTS’ historic halftime show, Global Citizen CEO Hugh Evans has publicly thanked the K-pop stars’ fans, after revealing ARMY has already raised more than $40,000 for children’s education. His message has shifted the focus away from the stage, showing how the next show is already impacting the real world weeks before kickoff.Global Citizen CEO Hugh Evans posted an emotional message and artwork of BTS members on social media, thanking the group and its global fanbase for supporting the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund.He said in the post that BTS will use the FIFA World Cup 2026 Final Halftime Show to highlight children’s education and encourage fans to donate to the campaign. “Even before the performance starts, ARMY has already made a meaningful difference,” Evans said.He said: "Seven absolutely incredible artists who chose to give back to children around the world. BTS is taking time away from their global tour to stand on the biggest stage in history. The first ever FIFA World Cup final halftime show on July 19. In support of the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund and our mission to raise 100 million dollars for children's education worldwide.""And ARMY, you haven't just waited for July 19 to show up. You've already raised over $40,000 for the fund. That's books, teachers, classrooms, safe places to play. That's children's whose lives are going to be changed because of you. BTS, thank you for choosing Purpose at this moment. For using your platform and your voices for kids who need it most. This community is one of the most powerful forces for good in the world. And on behalf of Global Citizen and the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund, we thank you. Thank you."Evans also posted separately that ARMY had already crossed the $40,000 milestone before July 19, calling the donations a direct investment in books, teachers, classrooms and opportunities for children worldwide.The upcoming FIFA World Cup final will make history by introducing the tournament's first-ever Super Bowl-style halftime show. The performance is scheduled for July 19 at MetLife Stadium, where football and music will share the spotlight during the championship match.BTS will headline the event alongside pop icon Madonna and Colombian superstar Shakira. The show is being curated by Coldplay frontman Chris Martin and is expected to support the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund, which aims to raise $100 million to improve access to education for children around the world.FIFA President Gianni Infantino previously described the event as an opportunity to bring together "music and football on the biggest stage in sport for a very special cause."For BTS, the performance marks another milestone in the group's World Cup journey. It comes four years after Jung Kook performed Dreamers during the opening ceremony of the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar. This time, all seven members will take the stage together in what is expected to be one of the most-watched live musical performances in history.As the countdown to July 19 continues, the conversation is extending far beyond entertainment. Thanks to BTS' involvement and the generosity of ARMY, the halftime show has already begun making an impact before a single note has been performed.
Article Summary: Global Citizen CEO Hugh Evans thanks BTS and ARMY. Reveals fans have already raised over $40,000 for the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund. The historic halftime show on July 19 will feature BTS, Madonna and Shakira, with proceeds helping the campaign's $100 million fundraising goal.

Article Title: Ronaldo effect? FIFA World Cup ticket prices crash after USA, Portugal exits
Article Text:
Portugal's Cristiano Ronaldo (7) (AP Photo)

The FIFA World Cup 2026 quarterfinals are set to begin this week, but ticket prices for one of the biggest knockout matches have dropped significantly following the elimination of co-host United States and Portugal.According to secondary marketplace TickPick, the get-in price for Friday's quarterfinal between Spain and Belgium has fallen dramatically from around $2,950 to nearly $1,200 as of Tuesday afternoon.The sharp decline comes after the United States suffered a 4-1 defeat to Belgium in the Round of 16, ending the co-hosts' World Cup campaign. A potential quarterfinal involving the home side would likely have generated much greater demand among fans.Portugal's exit also played a major role in the falling prices. Spain's 1-0 victory over Portugal not only ended Cristiano Ronaldo 's FIFA World Cup career but also removed one of the tournament's biggest global attractions from the knockout stages.A quarterfinal featuring the United States and Portugal had been viewed as one of the marquee possibilities of the tournament, with the presence of Ronaldo and the host nation expected to drive ticket demand even higher.Meanwhile, France's quarterfinal against Morocco at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough is currently the least expensive last-eight fixture on the secondary market. According to TickPick, tickets for the match are available from $989.The quarterfinals get underway on Friday with France taking on Morocco after Didier Deschamps' side edged Paraguay 1-0 in the Round of 16, while Morocco advanced with an emphatic 3-0 victory over co-hosts Canada.Spain will meet Belgium in the second quarterfinal after the Red Devils eliminated the United States 4-1, while Spain remain the only team yet to concede a goal in this year's tournament.Sunday's action begins with Erling Haaland's Norway facing Harry Kane-led England before defending champions Argentina take on Switzerland in the final quarterfinal.The four last-eight matches will be staged across Gillette Stadium in Foxborough, SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens and Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City, with the winners advancing to next week's semifinals.
Article Summary: FIFA World Cup 2026 quarterfinals set to begin this week. Ticket prices for one of the biggest knockout matches have dropped significantly following the elimination of co-hosts United States and Portugal. The get-in price for Friday's quarterfinal between Spain and Belgium has fallen dramatically from around $2,950 to nearly $1,200 as of Tuesday afternoon.

Article Title: 'How has Vaibhav Sooryavanshi not played?' : Ex-England captain baffled by India's decision
Article Text:
India's Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (ANI Photo)

Poll Vote & Share your view Who should be dropped to accommodate Sooryavanshi in the starting XI? Abhishek Sharma Sanju Samson 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

The excitement around Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has gone far beyond India, with the 15-year-old attracting attention from cricket fans across the world. Such has been the hype surrounding the teenage sensation that tickets for India's T20Is in Ireland were snapped up by supporters hoping to watch him in action. However, Sooryavanshi remained on the sidelines throughout the two-match series.The story did not change after India travelled to Durham for the T20Is against England. Despite the growing anticipation around his international debut, the youngster once again had to settle for a place on the bench. His continued omission has left several former cricketers puzzled, including Ravi Shastri and Sunil Gavaskar . Now, former England captains Michael Vaughan and Alastair Cook have also questioned India's decision.Speaking on the Stick to Cricket podcast, Vaughan admitted he could not understand why Sooryavanshi had not been given an opportunity, even suggesting he would like to put the question directly to the BCCI and chairman of selectors Ajit Agarkar.“We must say to the BCCI and the selector, Ajit, how has he not played against Ireland? Our mate, the Kid,” said Vaughan on the Stick to Cricket podcast, to which Cook replied: “I’m there tomorrow, and I’m driving up as a fan to make sure that the 15-year-old kid plays.”While Vaughan and Cook's comments reflected the growing curiosity over Sooryavanshi's absence, the chances of the Indian team management changing course appear slim. The selectors and coaching staff have consistently maintained that the teenager's opportunity will come at the appropriate time.At present, Sooryavanshi's path into the playing XI is blocked by Abhishek Sharma and Sanju Samson , both of whom remain firmly established in India's T20I side. Having played key roles in India's third T20 World Cup triumph four months ago, neither batter is expected to lose his place anytime soon.Even so, Sooryavanshi's omission has continued to spark debate considering the extraordinary rise he has enjoyed over the past five months. The left-hander first captained India Under-19 to the World Cup title, smashing a breathtaking 175 in the final. He then carried that momentum into the IPL, piling up 776 runs to become the youngest-ever Orange Cap winner.Before joining the senior squad for the Ireland tour, Sooryavanshi also impressed for India A in Sri Lanka. His match-winning 94 in the final included the fastest half-century in List A cricket and played a decisive role in India's comprehensive victory, further strengthening the case of those eager to see the teenage prodigy make his international debut.
Article Summary: India's Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is yet to make his international debut. The 15-year-old was left on the bench for India's T20Is against England. Michael Vaughan and Alastair Cook have questioned the selectors' decision. Abhishek Sharma and Sanju Samson remain firmly established in the side.

Article Title: 'Tougher days will come': Ex-India cricketer's reality check for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (Image: IANS)

India face England in Manchester

Former India wicketkeeper-batter Dinesh Karthik has lavished praise on teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, calling him a rare talent capable of rewriting T20 records, while also urging the youngster to stay grounded amid the growing spotlight.The 15-year-old batting prodigy has emerged as one of India's brightest prospects after his remarkable rise in domestic and IPL. He is likely to make his senior India debut during the ongoing T20I series against England, expectations around Sooryavanshi continue to soar.Speaking about the youngster's potential in Sky Sports, Karthik highlighted Sooryavanshi's fearless mindset and ability to score freely against any opposition."I think it's just his ability to score all around the ground and the tempo at which he bats. The fact that he doesn't get fazed by whoever his opponent is stands out. That fearlessness is a special quality," Karthik said.While acknowledging the teenager's immense talent, Karthik reminded everyone that every young cricketer goes through challenging phases, both on and off the field."Like everybody in life, I'm sure he will face challenges with his batting and things that happen away from cricket.He needs to learn how to deal with those situations. Right now, everything looks rosy, but tougher days will come," he added.Asked how far Sooryavanshi could go in the game, Karthik left little room for doubt."The sky is the limit. I genuinely feel he can become an extraordinary T20 batter, and I don't see why he can't break many records in the format," he said."My one piece of advice would be to learn to deal with the noise off the field in a healthy manner because that's going to be his biggest challenge in today's world," Karthik concluded.India and England will look to break the deadlock when they meet in the second T20I at Old Trafford, Manchester, after the series opener in Durham was washed out by rain. Despite the no result, India would have taken confidence from posting a competitive 189/7, powered by half-centuries from Abhishek Sharma and captain Shreyas Iyer.
Article Summary: Former India wicketkeeper-batter Dinesh Karthik has lavished praise on teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. The 15-year-old batting prodigy has emerged as one of India's brightest prospects. He is likely to make his senior India debut during the ongoing T20I series.

Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El Rubius was eliminated from MrBeast's $1 million streamer challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.

Article Title: IPL craze costs techie Rs 1.46 lakh in fake RCB vs CSK ticket scam
Article Text:
Image credit: AI generated pic

RCB register their second win

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you think the demand for IPL tickets is too high every season? Yes, it's excessive. No, it's just right. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: IPL comes and the demand for tickets and passes go soaring up by the fans. The desperation to get inside the stadium to watch their favourite teams in action in the IPL go high and high. When the tickets get sold out, the demand gets too hig, and the cricket fans even ask for tickets in black. But for a 25-year-old techie the craze for IPL tickets for RCB vs CSK turned too costly and he was duped for a massive Rs. 1.46 lakh.The techie, a resident of B Narayanapura, fell into a trap by a post posted by a fraudster who posed himself as a ticket seller on Instagram.As per a report in Deccan Herald, the techie alleged in a complaint to Mahadevapura Police that an individual claiming to be Sumit Biswal promised he could arrange IPL tickets along with food coupons.As per the techie, Biswal was introduced him as a senior supervisor for ticket counter at M Chinnaswamy Stadium"He asked me to come near the stadium gate number 10, saying someone would deliver the tickets within minutes, and even sent an email confirmation to gain my trust," the complainant told Deccan Herald."Initially, I agreed to buy two tickets for Rs 3,700 each,but he kept asking for more money under various pretexts such as refundable security deposits, additional ID cards and food coupons.Trusting him, I made multiple payments - even using my mother’s bank account after exhausting my own limits—and ended up transferring around Rs 1.46 lakh," he added.Bhuvneshwar Kumar’s incisive spell of 3-41, coming after a marauding batting show from Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) to a commanding 43-run victory over Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday.The contest saw RCB’s batting unit dismantle CSK’s attack with relentless hitting, as David’s 70 not out off just 25 balls and Patidar’s unbeaten 48 off only 19 balls provided for the late fireworks after Padikkal’s fluent 50 off 29 balls had set the tone for the daunting total.Their combined effort lifted RCB to a daunting 250/3, thus setting a new record for highest total in IPL 2026. In reply, CSK faltered under scoreboard pressure, with Sarfaraz Khan’s 50 off 24 balls and Prashant Veer’s 43 the lone acts of resistance amid a string of failures from the top order.Bhuvneshwar was at his disciplined best and even crossed the 200-mark in terms of wickets in IPL, with others also chipping in as CSK were bowled out for 207 in 19.4 overs. The comprehensive win also meant RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.
Article Summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) beat Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday. Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal put on a marauding batting show to lift RCB to a daunting 250/3. RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.

Article Title: Clavicular Viral Controversy: "Trans women are.....": Clavicular’s viral moment with trans women sparks fresh conversation on internet culture
Article Text:
A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Instead of conflict, the exchange highlights shared influence and mutual awareness. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Clavicular and trans women share a surprising realization about online trends







The video of Clavicular, a short-video producer, is going around the Internet, yet not the reason why. There is no yelling or over-the-top drama. It is a mere, everyday talk that seems almost novel nowadays, in the age of internet, which is overflowing with content. The scene is easy-going, the tone of the narrative is straightforward, and the characters appear more concerned with conversation than combat.The message that is being addressed is the flow of the conversation. It discusses internet culture, the way people communicate with one another and the place of trends. Rather than bringing out a debate, the encounter towards curiosity. It quietly brings attention to something that a lot of people who use the internet notice but don't often say out loud. People in different communities often have a bigger impact on each other than they think.The conversation begins with a confident and lighthearted comment from one of the trans women present. She says, "You know that trans women are like the OG lip masters, right? We are very talented in the program. Like, I do the peptides, I'm on Zeta." Her tone carries a mix of pride and humor, setting a relaxed vibe at the table.Clavicular responds in a way that keeps the mood steady but adds a different angle.He says, "That is funny because you guys were using a lot of the words in like the South Korean subreddits in like 2014." It’s not confrontational. If anything, it feels like an observation. He points to how certain words and ideas may have traveled across different online spaces over time.Another voice joins in and brings the moment full circle. "Guys, we're like the OGs. I saw a couple of posts about that. We're like copying each other." That line hits in a simple but honest way. It reflects something familiar. Online culture is rarely created in isolation. It grows through sharing, borrowing, and sometimes even unknowingly repeating.That's what makes the clip so powerful. It doesn't want to make a point or win an argument. It only shows a group of people talking, listening, and figuring something out together.This moment feels different in a space where viral content often leads to fights. It reminds people that conversations can be calm, thoughtful, and still important. And sometimes, that honest silence is what makes it work.
Article Summary: A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration.

Article Title: SIR search unearths Hindu student's AMU Seerat certificate
Article Text:
Delhi resident found an old school certificate while searching for electoral documents.

alif, be

"Some people would ask, 'Are you planning to become Muslim?' because I was participating in Seerat competitions,"

"But inside school, none of this felt unusual."

"Their first question was, 'Papa, what is Seerat?'"

"That's when I realised they were growing up in a very different world from the one I did,"





NEW DELHI: Across Delhi, steel almirahs are being opened, dusty trunks dragged out and cardboard boxes emptied as families search for birth certificates, school records and electoral slips amid the Election Commission's Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls.For 46-year-old Sharad Kumar Sharma, a senior manager at a Gurugram-based private company, the search unearthed something no official had asked for—a yellowing 1995 certificate from AMU City High School recognising him, a Hindu student, for representing his school in Seerat competitions on the life of Prophet Muhammad.Sharma had begun searching after failing to trace his parents' names on electoral rolls from the period when his family moved from Aligarh to Delhi in 2002-03. The certificate, browned with age, bears the AMU seal, the blue-ink signature of then principal Badrul Islam, and a line that caught his attention after three decades: "Being a non-Muslim student he has represented this school through his participation in Seerat competitions (Character of Mohammad Prophet). ..""I had completely forgotten about it," Sharma said. "I wasn't looking for this. I was looking for documents."The discovery prompted him to reconnect with former classmates, including a journalist and an anaesthetist, who had studied Urdu and participated in similar competitions alongside him.Their conversations soon turned nostalgic, recalling a period when Hindu parents routinely hired Urdu tutors to prepare their children for admission to AMU, where elementary Urdu formed part of the entrance process and school curriculum."Back then, if you wanted to study at AMU, you learnt Urdu," Sharma said. "Hindu families, including mine, hired tutors because admission mattered more than anything else. Nobody found that unusual."One of those classmates, now a journalist with a national daily, remembers the experience similarly. "I scored better in compulsory Urdu than in advanced Hindi," he said, adding that elementary Urdu remains common among many non-Muslim students in Aligarh because the city's education system continues to revolve around AMU. "It starts as a necessity for good education and then some develop an interest."Sharma's memories are echoed by Parveen Jahan, now in her late 60s, who taught Urdu to dozens of children from her home in Aligarh's Upper Kot neighbourhood for years."Hindu parents came because their children wanted admission to AMU," she said. "Advanced Hindi students still had to study elementary Urdu."She recalls one of her brightest pupils, Surbhi, a Hindu Class X student. "We started with—the Urdu alphabet," Jahan said. "In her first test, she scored 35 out of 100. Within six months, she was reading Iqbal and Ghalib. When she passed, she came home to show me her result."According to Jahan, Hindu students still come to learn Urdu, though fewer than before. "The interest is still there because of the university," she said. "Sikhs came too. Parents wanted their children to learn." However, she said she no longer sees Hindu students taking part in Seerat competitions as they once did.For Sharma, Urdu and Seerat competitions were simply part of growing up in Aligarh. He remembers volunteering for public-speaking contests, including one at AMU's Kennedy Hall where he was the only non-Muslim participant among the finalists.Outside campus, however, there were occasional questions.Sharma recalled.The certificate took on new meaning only after he showed it to his children.Sharma said.The family is still searching for documents that may help establish their electoral records from the early 2000s. The AMU certificate has gone back into the same cardboard file where it lay unnoticed for nearly three decades.It may not help prove identity or residency.But amid a city-wide search for official records, it reopened memories of an educational culture in which Hindu children learnt Urdu, participated in Seerat competitions and carried home certificates that, at the time, seemed ordinary enough to be forgotten.
Article Summary: Delhi resident finds old school certificate while searching for electoral documents. Certificate recognised him for representing school in Seerat competitions. Hindu parents routinely hired Urdu tutors to prepare children for admission to AMU. Urdu remains common among many non-Muslim students in Aligarh.

Article Title: Theatre review: A macabre Shakespearean tragedy gets a comic makeover
Article Text:
Still from Macbeth: What’s Done Is Done

Macbeth

Macbeth: What’s Done Is Done

Should you watch it?





Centred on the themes of regicide, betrayal and tyranny,is one of Shakespeare’s darkest tragedies. In contrast, Rajat Kapoor ’s satirical adaptation adds a layer of slapstick comedy to the sombre classic. While in the original, Shakespeare uses the opening scene to set the eerie tone of the play with three mysterious witches, Kapoor’s version replaces them with two neat, white-faced clowns, Julio and Pedro, who invite the audience into the absurd world of Macky B and his Lady through the device of a play within a play.The two comic characters, who present themselves as the producers of the metaplay, deftly weave the parallel narratives into a compelling tale of ambition, guilt and moral decay, complete with wit and humour. Despite the tonal shift in Kapoor’s reimagining,, the protagonists’ psychological unravelling remains firmly at the heart of the story. Macky B’s fall from grace begins when he and his friend Banquo encounter the three witches, referred to as the ‘weird sisters’, in the forest.Their prophecies plant the seed of ambition in his mind, luring him towards his tragic fate. Ranvir Shorey is compelling as the once-loyal servant of the king of Scotland, charting Macky B’s transformation from a nobleman consumed by greed and guilt into a ruthless, immoral usurper.Throughout the play, the witches lurk like dark thoughts and unconscious temptations to evil, driving the narrative of enigmatic prophecies, brutal murders and spiralling madness.They foretell that Macky B will become the Thane of Cawdor and eventually King of Scotland, while Banquo’s descendants will inherit the throne, setting in motion a bloody chain of ambition, paranoia and destruction. After Banquo’s murder, the witches lure Macky B into a false sense of invincibility with cryptic prophecies. Blinded by overconfidence, he unleashes a ruthless wave of violence until the prophecies turn against him, leading to his downfall.Want us to review your play? Mail us at tims.delhitimes @timesofindia.com
Article Summary: Rajat Kapoor’s satirical adaptation adds a layer of slapstick comedy to the sombre classic. The two comic characters deftly weave the parallel narratives into a compelling tale of ambition, guilt and moral decay. Ranvir Shorey is compelling as the once-loyal servant of the king of Scotland.
